segments.text: Brethren and fathers, hear my defense before you now.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And when they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, they kept all the more silent.
Then he said:

I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, taught according to the strictness of our fathers' law, and was zealous toward God as you all are today.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: as also the high priest bears me witness, and all the council of the elders, from whom I also received letters to the brethren, and went to Damascus to bring in chains even those who were there to Jerusalem to be punished.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Now it happened, as I journeyed and came near Damascus at about noon, suddenly a great light from heaven shone around me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me,
Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Who are You, Lord?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they did not hear the voice of Him who spoke to me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: What shall I do, Lord?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Arise and go into Damascus, and there you will be told all things which are appointed for you to do.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And since I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came into Damascus.
Then a certain Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good testimony with all the Jews who dwelt there,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: came to me; and he stood and said to me,
Brother Saul, receive your sight.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: And at that same hour I looked up at him.
The God of our fathers has chosen you that you should know His will, and see the Just One, and hear the voice of His mouth.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: For you will be His witness to all men of what you have seen and heard.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: And now why are you waiting? Arise and be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: Now it happened, when I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, that I was in a trance
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Make haste and get out of Jerusalem quickly, for they will not receive your testimony concerning Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, they know that in every synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believe on You.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And when the blood of Your martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by consenting to his death, and guarding the clothes of those who were killing him.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Depart, for I will send you far from here to the Gentiles.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they listened to him until this word, and then they raised their voices and said,
Away with such a fellow from the earth, for he is not fit to live!
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Then, as they cried out and tore off their clothes and threw dust into the air,
the commander ordered him to be brought into the barracks, and said that he should be examined under scourging, so that he might know why they shouted so against him.
Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman, and uncondemned?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: When the centurion heard that, he went and told the commander, saying,
Take care what you do, for this man is a Roman.
segments.speaker: Centurion

segments.text: Then the commander came and said to him,
Tell me, are you a Roman?
segments.speaker: Commander

segments.text: Yes.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: With a large sum I obtained this citizenship.
segments.speaker: Commander

segments.text: But I was born a citizen.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Then immediately those who were about to examine him withdrew from him; and the commander was also afraid after he found out that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.

The next day, because he wanted to know for certain why he was accused by the Jews, he released him from his bonds, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down and set him before them.
